documents.title: THE HOLLOW BLADE
documents.subtitle: Artifact Profile — Class V Restricted — Recovery Priority: MAXIMUM

sections.heading: DESCRIPTION
sections.body:
A blade of unknown alloy, approximately 38cm in length. Weight: 40% less than predicted for its volume and apparent density. The material is not identifiable by any existing spectrographic method.

Carriers report persistent auditory phenomena: 'a sound like whispering,' 'someone counting,' 'reading names.' The sounds are inaudible to others. Three carriers have attempted to transcribe what they hear. The transcriptions do not share any identifiable language. They do not share any identifiable structure. Two of the three carriers subsequently requested reassignment from Sector 9. The third is unavailable for follow-up.

sections.heading: ORIGIN
sections.body:
Recovered from Relay Station Omicron-7 during a pre-collapse survey, Cycle 4415.1. Location of recovery: embedded in the wall of Sector 3, data vault access corridor. Not inserted. Not dropped. Embedded — as if the wall had grown around it, or as if it had arrived inside the wall from some other direction.

No documentation predates this recovery. Attempts to identify the alloy's composition through historical materials databases returned no matches.

sections.heading: THE SABLE THRESHOLD INCIDENT
sections.body:
Operative Hask deployed the Hollow Blade against the Pallid Watcher in Sector 2 of Omicron-7 during Operation Sable Threshold.

The Blade's resonance effect — a high-frequency void emission that has been documented to disrupt Class I-III entities — was received by the Watcher, modified in real time, inverted, and reflected back at the operative team.

Hask, Solari, and Brunt died. The Blade was dropped. Its last detected signal placed it in Sector 3, in the data vault access corridor — the same location where it was originally found.

This is where it started. This is where it went back.

We do not know what to do with this information.

sections.heading: CURRENT STATUS
sections.body:
The Hollow Blade is in Relay Station Omicron-7, Sector 3, presumably in or near the data vault access corridor.

The Pallid Watcher is maintaining active occupation of the station.

Recovery Priority: MAXIMUM.

Operational Feasibility: the committee has met four times. No proposal has been approved. We continue to maintain the MAXIMUM priority designation while acknowledging that we have no current plan to act on it.

This is not unusual. Priority designations and resource allocation are separate processes.
